name: Mariachi Beefballs and Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, lightly combine ground beef with corn chips, milk, beaten egg, and 1 teaspoon salt. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Shape into 15 meatballs, using about 2 rounded tablespoons of the meat mixture for each. Lightly roll meatballs in 2 tablespoons flour, coating completely.
3. In a large frying pan, cook meatballs in hot butter, half at a time, stirring until evenly browned. Remove meatballs from the pan as they are browned.
4. In the same frying pan, cook onion and garlic for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. In a small bowl, combine remaining 1/2 tablespoon flour and 1 teaspoon salt, chili powder, and cumin. Stir into onions.
6. Add tomatoes, green chilies, and olives. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly; reduce heat, cover tightly, and cook slowly for 30 minutes.
7. Add meatballs to tomato mixture, cover tightly, and cook slowly for 20 minutes.
8. Uncover and continue cooking slowly for an additional 10 minutes.
9. Serve meatballs and sauce over hot Mexican Rice.
Mexican Rice:
1. In a separate bowl, combine sour cream, Monterey Jack cheese, chopped green chilies, and salt.
2. In a 13 x 9-inch baking pan, layer 1 cup cooked rice, 1/2 of the sour cream-chili mixture, and 1/2 of the cheese strips.
3. Repeat layers and top with remaining cup of rice.
4. Bake in a moderate oven (350 degrees F.) for 25 minutes.
5. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and place on meatballs and sauce. Continue baking for an additional 5 minutes or until cheese melts.
